title: New here with a newbie question
display_title: New here with a newbie question
wikitext:
[]: New here with a newbie question             Quote Post by andrewyi1114 » Thu Apr 16, 2015 1:47 pm I recently bought a Korean boxset (Renewal) and the movies on DVD and had some quick questions. Now it's generally stated online that Death is a re-edit of the first 24 episodes with new scenes added that were later implemented on the DC episodes for 21-24. However I just watched the Renewal episodes and this is my first time watching Death and noticed a couple of things. Is the horrible widescreen crop this DVD's fault or is that how they aired it in theaters? And I'm pretty sure there are some scenes that are not present in the DC. This is only my second time rewatching the series and the first time that I'm watching Death so I can't be a 100% sure, but even in the first scene there's a cut of the expedition where Misato's father puts her in a big tube and saves her. And then a little bit later there's a scene of Shinji playing cello. Now I'm pretty positive I've never seen these scenes before. I have yet to watch End of Evangelion so maybe they end up there? or are they only in Death?? I apologize if this is a stupid question, but there doesn't seem to be any explanation for these scenes on the wiki.

[]: Re: New here with a newbie question             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 16, 2015 1:53 pm andrewyi1114 wrote: Is the horrible widescreen crop this DVD's fault or is that how they aired it in theaters?

[]:             Quote Post by andrewyi1114 » Fri Apr 17, 2015 10:55 am Ok now I'm more confused. I saw this on here [url]http://evangelion.wikia.com/wiki/Death_and_Rebirth[/url] "Death was later re-edited when Death & Rebirth premiered on the Japanese "WOWOW" satellite TV network, and renamed Death(true), which omitted much of the new footage. Death was finalized with the release of the film Revival of Evangelion, and named Death(true)². This version is identical to Death(true) with the exception of a couple of new shots never before seen in either prior incarnation (this new footage is also integrated into the Japanese Laserdisc and the American and European Platinum Collection releases of the series). " So Death(true)2 has scenes that aren't in the original Death?? And in either case neither the director's cut nor the Renewal versions of episodes 21' - 24' have these scenes? So ultimately I need to watch Death(true)2 for anything I've missed. (I don't have the platinum collection, I have a Korean DVD of death so I'm not sure if it' Death or Death(true)2) And what is the deal with Rebirth episodes 25' 26' and End of Evangelion?? Episodes 25' and 26' are identical the End of Evangelion it says?? " "Air" is episode 25' of Neon Genesis Evangelion. It aired as a corresponding "replacement" episode for Episode 25; along with Episode 26', the two combined to make up The End of Evangelion. " So I know Rebirth the couldn't finish so they did 25' and 26' redoing a lot of scenes. But were there any changes made when making the End of Evangelion?? I'm getting so confused EDIT: After looking through those threads you linked me to I think I've reached a sort of conclusion. The original release of Death was only theatrical and never released on home video, and Death(true) was on a Japanese cable network without the extra scenes, so the DVD I have must be Death(true)2 or whatever is available via home media. So in any case there's nothing in the original Death that is missing from Death(true)2 and I've been fussing about nothing for no reason. I will finish watching it and End of Evangelion AND episodes 25' 26' (still not sure if these are different) and stop obsessing over this like I should've done in the first place. Thanks for your help

[]:             Quote Post by The Cruel » Fri Apr 17, 2015 8:33 pm To get things straight what type of versions of NGE and the Movies we have, I have something and in chronological order. NGE itself: Simply the basic which aired at first from autumn 95 to spring 96 on TV without any extensions or improvments made until later releases on VHS, LD, DVD and the upcomming BD Box Set. DEATH & REBIRTH : Released in spring 97, the film that was actually meant to conclude the story of NGE and having the ending which should make more "sense" than the last two Episodes 25 and 26. But because Gainax hasn't completed anything yet, the rest of the story was shown later with EoE. Its split in two parts which makes this film into a double feature: DEATH: The recap of the whole series until Ep 24 with some new scenes that revealed some mysteries of the series and put later stand-alone in the home video releases of the series. REBIRTH: Actually being the ending for NGE, while Gainax was still working on this, this part of the film is literary a large ca. 25 min. preview from Ep 25': Air/Love is destructive from the real deal EoE which ended with the arival of the MP-Evas. Shortly before Revival of Evangelion was released, an edited version of this film caled DEATH(true)&REBIRTH aired on TV excluding all the new footage. The home video version of DEATH&REBITH had DEATH(true)². End of Evangelion Released four months later after DEATH & REBIRTH in summer of 97 and being the final ending, replacing the last two episodes. Bizarrely this movie released one day before my thrid birthday. No more words need to be said except for its structure. Despite being a movie, it's mostly built up like some tv episodes seperated by the credit roll in the middle: Ep 25': Air/Love is destructive and Ep 26': My Purest Heart to You/I Need You The first home video release of EoE for VHS/LD/DVD which had the last two episodes of NGE too were built up more like some traditional tv Episodes, having also some short previews and their own credit rolls. Revival of Evangelion In the spring of 98, Almost released one year after DEATH & REBITH, Revival of Evangelion is literary the final version of the said film, which has an visualy improved and a little extended version of DEATH, called DEATH(true)² and replacing REBITH with EoE. New Production Cut Episodes aka Directors Cut Episodes While Revival of Evangelion was released in 98, durring the same year for the home video releases of NGE, the Episodes 21-24 were replaced with the extended and visually improved versions called Episodes 21'-24'. Storywisely the new scenes for 21'-24' are from DEATH but also having new scenes on their own. Overal though you can see that DEATH and the DC Episodes are meant to bind NGE with EoE This makes four ways how to watch NGE 1. NGE 2. NGE - Ep 25,26 + DEATH & REBIRTH + EoE 3. NGE - Ep 25,26 + Revival of Evangelion 4. NGE - Ep 21-26 + Ep 21'-24' + EoE I hope this can help you.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 17, 2015 10:26 pm The Cruel wrote: New Production Cut Episodes aka Directors Cut Episodes

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 17, 2015 11:05 pm You left out the people who think EoE and 25-26 both happen at the same time. but yeah, good summary otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by The Cruel » Sat Apr 18, 2015 1:23 am NemZ wrote: You left out the people who think EoE and 25-26 both happen at the same time. but yeah, good summary otherwise.

Article: New here with a newbie question
 NGE and the Movies we have, I have something and in chronological order. NGE itself: Simply the basic which aired at first from autumn 95 to spring 96 on TV without any extensions or improvments made until later releases on VHS, LD, DVD and the upcomming BD Box Set. DEATH & REBIRTH : Released in spring 97, the film that was actually meant to conclude the story of NGE and having the ending which should make more "sense" than the last two Episodes 25 and 26. But because Gainax hasn't completed anything yet, the rest of the story was shown later with EoE. Its split in two parts which makes this film into a double feature: DEATH: The recap of the whole series until Ep 24 with some new scenes that revealed some mysteries of the series and put later stand-alone in the home video releases of the series. REBIRTH: Actually being the ending for NGE, while Gainax was still working on this, this part of the film is literary a large ca. 25 min. preview from Ep 25': Air/Love is destructive from the real deal EoE which ended with the arival of the MP-Evas. Shortly before Revival of Evangelion was released, an edited version of this film caled DEATH(true)&REBIRTH aired on TV excluding all the new footage. The home video version of DEATH&REBITH had DEATH(true)². End of Evangelion Released four months later after DEATH & REBIRTH in summer of 97 and being the final ending, replacing the last two episodes. Bizarrely this movie released one day before my thrid birthday. No more words need to be said except for its structure. Article: New here with a newbie question
 the spring of 98, Almost released one year after DEATH & REBITH, Revival of Evangelion is literary the final version of the said film, which has an visualy improved and a little extended version of DEATH, called DEATH(true)² and replacing REBITH with EoE. New Production Cut Episodes aka Directors Cut Episodes While Revival of Evangelion was released in 98, durring the same year for the home video releases of NGE, the Episodes 21-24 were replaced with the extended and visually improved versions called Episodes 21'-24'. Storywisely the new scenes for 21'-24' are from DEATH but also having new scenes on their own. Overal though you can see that DEATH and the DC Episodes are meant to bind NGE with EoE This makes four ways how to watch NGE 1. NGE 2. NGE - Ep 25,26 + DEATH & REBIRTH + EoE 3. NGE - Ep 25,26 + Revival of Evangelion 4. NGE - Ep 21-26 + Ep 21'-24' + EoE I hope this can help you.
